name: Employee Confidentiality Obligation
text: The Employee shall, during the term of employment and indefinitely thereafter, maintain strict confidentiality with respect to all Confidential Information of the Employer. 'Confidential Information' means all non-public information relating to the Employer's business, clients, products, services, technology, financial data, business plans, trade secrets, and know-how that the Employee may acquire in the course of employment, whether in written, oral, electronic, or any other form. The Employee shall: (a) not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the Employer; (b) use Confidential Information solely for the purposes of carrying out the Employee's duties; (c) promptly notify the Employer upon becoming aware of any actual or suspected unauthorised disclosure; and (d) upon termination of employment, immediately return all documents and materials containing Confidential Information and permanently delete all electronic copies. Post-employment confidentiality obligations shall survive termination to the extent they relate to trade secrets.
invalid_if: Post-employment confidentiality for non-trade-secret information exceeds reasonable period
Confidential Information definition is so broad as to cover publicly available information
title: Employee Confidentiality
dispute_hotspots: Employee takes client list — trade secret theft; injunction available
SEBI UPSI — employee trades on insider information; regulatory prosecution
